title: ericameria viscidiflora subsp. puberula
intro: Ericameria viscidiflora subsp. puberula, also known as puberula greasewood or greasewood, is a subspecies of shrub that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, and is known for its small, yellow flowers and sticky leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, and is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating skin conditions. Ericameria viscidiflora subsp. puberula can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
